refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analysis of terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and solution neutrality, these should be separated into distinct FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis without implying any implementation details.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain of the worksite area independently of other features.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Extract and represent key site features relevant to planning and safety (e.g., obstacles, zones)
Integrate terrain and site features into a unified worksite map model
Provide an interface to share the worksite model with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Integrate terrain and site features into a unified worksite map model

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and site features into a single model may create dependencies between data sources
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically groups terrain representation and site features into one model, supporting independence from planning/execution functions by providing a clear interface.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate terrain and site feature data
Risk that integration complexity may increase if site features are highly dynamic or heterogeneous
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the unified model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating terrain modeling and site feature extraction as distinct DPs if their data sources or update rates differ significantly, to reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Contains the logic and data integration for terrain and site features into a unified worksite map model within the overall solution

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor data and sends processed map updates

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communicates map data and updates with other system components

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the NGHE Solution is responsible for data integration and mapping; minimal diffusion as map data is shared externally but processing is centralized.
Vehicle module uses map data but does not integrate terrain/site features; would require significant diffusion to contain DP.
Teleoperation consumes map data for remote control but does not perform integration; poor containment.
Safety Services may use map data for alerts but is not responsible for integration; very weak containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary to contain the DP as it logically integrates terrain and site features into a unified map, centralizing processing and minimizing diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential diffusion of map data to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may increase coupling.
If NGHE Solution grows too large, it may become a bottleneck or single point of failure.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: If containment risks increase, consider decomposing the NGHE Solution into submodules separating data integration and map dissemination to improve modularity without losing cohesion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP clearly addresses the FR by specifying the integration of terrain and site features into a unified worksite map model, which fulfills the requirement to provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.

diffusion_risks: Map data diffusion to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may increase coupling and risk of inconsistent data handling.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high cohesion and centralizes the processing and integration of terrain and site features, minimizing diffusion. Other modules consume map data but do not perform integration, indicating good containment of the DP within NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module produces a unified worksite map model that can be validated against acceptance criteria requiring availability of the model to planning and execution functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution module is clear and logical, with well-defined interfaces for data input and output, supporting traceability from FR to IM.

recommended_revisions: Monitor the growth of the NGHE Solution module to avoid it becoming a bottleneck or single point of failure.
Consider decomposing the NGHE Solution into submodules separating data integration and map dissemination if containment risks increase, to improve modularity without losing cohesion.
Manage the diffusion risk by clearly defining interfaces and data ownership between NGHE Solution and consuming modules like Vehicle and Teleoperation.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is well addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The NGHE Solution module centralizes integration with good cohesion and manageable diffusion risks. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to monitor the module growth and diffusion risks as recommended, but no immediate further decomposition is necessary at this stage.

revise.why: Monitor the NGHE Solution module for potential growth and diffusion risks; consider future decomposition into submodules if modularity or containment issues arise.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can maintain high cohesion and manageable diffusion as system evolves.
Interfaces between NGHE Solution and consuming modules remain well-defined and stable.
open_risks: Potential for NGHE Solution module to become a bottleneck or single point of failure if it grows too large.
Risk of data diffusion causing inconsistent handling across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Monitoring data on module size and complexity over time.
Verification results confirming unified worksite map model availability and correctness.
Interface definitions and data ownership agreements between modules.
